sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports finger pain following a skiing accident five days ago, rated as a 7/10. He took ibuprofen without significant relief. He denies any other injuries sustained during the incident. The patient also reports chronic constipation and takes miralax. He had an appendectomy at age seven. He reports no other surgeries.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The right index finger x-ray demonstrates no fractures or bony abnormalities. Physical examination reveals tenderness to palpation, pain with joint movement, and pain with compression of the DIP joint. Vital signs are within normal limits.
medical_concepts: pain with compression of the DIP joint

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a sprain of the distal interphalangeal joint of the right index finger. This is supported by the physical exam and x-ray findings.
medical_concepts: sprain of the distal interphalangeal joint of the right index finger

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe a strong antiinflammatory medication, mobic, at 15 mg once daily. Order a splint for the right index finger. Schedule a follow-up appointment in two weeks to assess healing. Arrange for hand therapy if needed at that time. Dragon, please order the medications and procedures mentioned.

medical_concepts: pain with compression of the DIP joint
sprain of the distal interphalangeal joint of the right index finger
